_schema_note: jpods-trip-v2 DRAFT — 2026-05-20. Complete navigation package. Written by TripPlanner after reading map.json v2. Self-contained: Nora never opens map.json mid-trip. Same format for SketchUp simulator and physical Pi dispatch. Allie and Noelle: annotate _innovation fields with observations and candidates for next iteration.
_note_map_version: TripPlanner records the schema of the map.json it read. Nora can detect if trip was built from a different map version than the current map.json.
_innovation_global: ALLIE: trip.json is also Alice's billing input. Should it carry passenger_count, cargo_kg, requested_by (carryon_uuid)?
ALLIE: a trip.json generated in SketchUp and transferred to the Pi is a pre-computed route. Should it carry an 'expires_at' field so stale pre-computed trips are not executed?
NOELLE: should TripPlanner validate that every segment in the trip exists in the current map.json before writing? Catch topology drift before Nora sees it.
NOELLE: consider a 'trip_hash' field — SHA of the segment id sequence. Nora can verify the trip she received matches what Dispatcher sent. Tamper detection.
ALLIE: Route-Time needs O-D matrices. trip.json is the natural source — TripPlanner writes one file per O-D pair, Route-Time reads the set. No separate route computation needed.

trips._note_total_length: Sum of all segment length_mm values. TripPlanner calculates at write time. Nora uses for progress reporting (mm_traveled / total_length_mm).
trips._note_estimated_time: INNOVATION CANDIDATE: nominal travel time at average speed. TripPlanner calculates from segment length_mm / speedMax for each segment. Alice uses for schedule. Dispatcher uses for headway planning.
trips._note_estimated_energy: INNOVATION CANDIDATE: sum of energy_model.estimated_wh across all segments from map.json. Alice bills by energy. Solar offset credited against this.
trips._note_passenger_capacity: From station template — declared in noelle_features.json. Alice enforces booking limit.

segments._note: First intra-station segment. Pod departs S048 platform. All physical data embedded from map.json — Nora reads this record, navigates, never queries map.json.
segments._note_cumulative_mm: Distance from trip start to beginning of this segment. Nora reports position as cumulative_mm + mm_into_segment.
segments._note_action: INNOVATION CANDIDATE: depart | transit | arrive | dwell. Nora uses to trigger door open/close, passenger notification, cargo lock/unlock. TripPlanner derives from role.
segments._note_dwell_time_s: Non-zero only for gw_platform at origin (boarding) and destination (alighting). TripPlanner sets from noelle_features.json dwell_time_s.

segments._note_endPoint: Stub pair tip — this is the CP. Connects to inter-station segment startPoint.

segments._note_grade: Stub pair lifts from station deck to guideway beam height — significant grade. Nora needs extra motor torque here.

segments._note: Abbreviated — full Bezier path has ~212 segments at 2m resolution. Noelle generates all from followme.json track data.

ezones._note: INNOVATION CANDIDATE: ezones relevant to this segment, copied from map.json at plan time. Nora checks these without opening map.json.

segments._note_endPoint: Populated by Noelle from map.json. Placeholder zeros here in draft.

segments._note: Terminal segment. Pod stops here. speedMax 50 = slow crawl into platform. dwell_time_s 30 = passenger alighting + boarding window.

physical_json_check._note: INNOVATION CANDIDATE: before Dispatcher assigns this trip to Nora, check physical.json for known anomalies on any segment. Noelle reads physical.json; if severity >= moderate on any segment in the trip, block the assignment and flag to operator.
